title: Cross-Environment Multi-Paradigm Motor Imagery EEG Dataset
description: This dataset comprises EEG recordings from 84 healthy participants performing motor imagery tasks across two controlled environments (laboratory and simulated hospital), with a supplementary cohort of 3 participants recorded in microgravity conditions aboard a space station. The main dataset includes multiple motor imagery paradigms (Graz, SSMVEP-MI, and hybrid variants) with 69-channel EEG recordings (64-channel system with 60 channels used plus 9 derived channels) at 1000 Hz sampling rate, designed to investigate environmental effects on brain-computer interface performance and enable cross-environment robustness evaluation. The space station supplementary dataset employs a different acquisition setup (EGGO system with 32 dry electrodes) and is provided as a separate resource due to its distinct methodology and limited sample size.
methods_description: Main Dataset: EEG data were collected using a 64-channel system following the 10-20 electrode placement system with 60 channels used in acquisition and 9 additional derived channels included in analysis (69 channels total). Recordings were acquired at 1000 Hz sampling rate in two distinct environments: electromagnetically shielded laboratory and simulated hospital with multi-sensory elements. Motor imagery tasks included left-hand, right-hand, and feet movements with standardized trial structure (cue: -2 to 0 s, task: 0 to 4 s, rest: 4 s). Data were organized in BIDS format. Space Station Supplementary Dataset: Employed an EGGO system with 32 dry electrodes in microgravity conditions, stored as MATLAB files.

keywords.term: SSMVEP-MI (Steady-State Motor Imagery Visual Evoked Potential)
